title: EB1A evidence tracker
banner: USCIS applies a two-step analysis (criteria + final merits). Meeting 3 criteria on paper is the floor, not the bar. Build with an attorney.

criteria.title: Nationally or internationally recognized awards
criteria.hint: Industry awards, hackathon wins with national reach, fellowship selections. Internal company awards rarely count alone.

criteria.title: Membership in associations requiring outstanding achievement
criteria.hint: Senior/Fellow grades (e.g. IEEE Senior Member), invite-only bodies with documented selectivity.

criteria.title: Published material about you
criteria.hint: Articles about you and your work in professional/major media — not authored by you.

criteria.title: Judging the work of others
criteria.hint: Peer review, hackathon/competition judging, program committees, patent review panels.

criteria.title: Original contributions of major significance
criteria.hint: The heavyweight criterion: adoption metrics, citations, letters from independent experts, industry impact.

criteria.title: Scholarly articles
criteria.hint: Peer-reviewed papers, or substantive trade/professional publications you authored.

criteria.title: Artistic exhibitions or showcases
criteria.hint: Mostly for arts fields — skip unless genuinely applicable.

criteria.title: Leading or critical role for distinguished organizations
criteria.hint: Org charts, letters showing your role was critical — for units with documented distinction.

criteria.title: High remuneration relative to the field
criteria.hint: Comp data vs BLS/levels.fyi percentiles for your occupation and region.

criteria.title: Commercial success in performing arts
criteria.hint: Box office / streaming / sales numbers — arts fields only.

sources.title: USCIS Policy Manual — EB-1A extraordinary ability
